items.title: MOF: Health Ministry spending adjustment capped at RM500m, not RM3.1b as claimed online
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — The Ministry of Finance (MOF) has considered an adjustment to the Health Ministry’s operating expenditure of up to RM500 million, far below the RM3.1 billion figure circulating on social media, said Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan. He said the amount does not involve core spending, including emoluments, medical supplies, overtime payments and on-call allowances. Amir Hamzah said the expenditure adjustment only covers non-critical operating costs, while all core spending, including essential services, security, healthcare and education, would continue as approved under Budget 2026.

items.title: Woman found stuffed in suitcase died from slash wounds to the head, say police
items.summary: KAJANG: The unidentified woman whose body was found inside a suitcase in a home in Taman Impian Indah here on Sunday died from slash wounds to the head. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said this was confirmed following a post-mortem conducted at the Kajang Hospital Forensic Unit at 6.20pm yesterday. Yesterday, it was reported that police received a report on the discovery of an unidentified body at a house in Taman Impian Indah at 8.37pm on Sunday.

items.title: Woman jailed, daughter and son-in-law claim trial over beer theft
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: A 50-year-old woman was jailed for four months by the magistrate's court here today after pleading guilty to stealing three cartons of beer with her daughter and son-in-law last month. Tamil Selvi, 50, entered the plea after the charge was read to her before Magistrate Aini Adilah Muhammad Faizal. Her daughter, N. Tawamani, 23, and son-in-law, V. Thayaran, 26, pleaded not guilty to the same charge.

items.title: From massage to magistrate: Kota Kinabalu therapist in hot water over 'inappropriate' bodywork
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 30 — A 31-year-old massage therapist was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here to molesting a male client during a body massage session. The charge under Section 354 of the Penal Code carries a jail term of up to 10 years, or a fine, or whipping, or any two of the punishments, on conviction. The court fixed Aug 5 for pre-trial case management and released the accused on bail of RM3,000 with two sureties.

items.title: Penang plans slaughterhouses in every district to boost livestock industry
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang plans to establish a slaughterhouse in every district to strengthen the state's livestock industry and enhance food safety standards. State Rural Development, Agrotechnology, Food Security and Entrepreneurship Committee chairman Datuk Rashidi Zinol said the initiative is aimed at creating a more organised, hygienic and syariah-compliant slaughtering ecosystem while supporting the state's modern and sustainable agri-food agenda. He said the proposed district slaughterhouses would be developed through strategic collaboration between the state government, the Penang Veterinary Services Department, the local government division under state Local Government and Town and Country Planning Committee chairman Jason H'ng Mooi Lye and the Penang Islamic Religious Affairs Department under Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Dr Mohamad Abdul Hamid.

items.title: Dewan Rakyat passes Sexual Offences Against Children amendment Bill 2026, closing overseas loopholes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — The Dewan Rakyat today passed the Sexual Offences Against Children (Amendment) Bill 2026, extending the Act’s jurisdiction to cover offences committed outside Malaysia. Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) M. Kulasegaran said the amendment to expand extraterritorial application will ensure that offenders with ties to Malaysia cannot evade justice simply because their crimes were committed overseas. This amendment aims to prevent overlapping proceedings and ensure that offenders do not slip through the legal net,” he said.

items.title: Two soldiers injured by debris during weapons training at Hobart Camp
items.summary: ALOR STAR: Two soldiers were injured after being struck by debris during a weapons training exercise at Hobart Camp here yesterday. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the incident occurred at 6.05pm while the victims, a sergeant and a corporal, were taking part in a multi-weapon training session involving other participants. "The 29-year-old trainee, a corporal, sustained left knee injuries.

items.title: Shell expects 65pct rise in global LNG demand by 2050
items.summary: LONDON/SINGAPORE: Global liquefied natural gas demand is expected to rise by around 65 per cent by 2050, driven largely by Asia as countries seek lower-emission alternatives to coal and data centres boost power demand, Shell said in an annual report on Tuesday. Global demand is likely to reach nearly 700 million metric tons a year by that date, the world's largest trader of the superchilled fuel said in its 2026 LNG Outlook. The US-Israeli war on Iran has disrupted the global LNG outlook, driving up prices, damaging Qatar's export facilities and delaying new supply, casting doubt on demand from price-sensitive Asian buyers.

items.title: Petronas strikes new oil discoveries in Suriname
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas), through its unit Petronas Suriname E&P BV, has made two new discoveries and completed a successful appraisal at offshore Block 52 in Suriname. The Swartzia Aspasia Complex-1 (SAC-1) exploration well, located eight kilometres east of Sloanea-1 gas discovery in 610 metres of water depth, and drilled to a total depth of 4,560 metres, intersected gas-bearing sandstone reservoirs. Petronas said the drill stem testing (DST) demonstrated strong gas deliverability, which indicates good reservoir quality.

items.title: Zambry: LRT3 to ease commute, cut costs for university students
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The newly opened Shah Alam LRT3 line will make commuting easier and more affordable for university students while helping to ease traffic congestion. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir, who travelled on the LRT3 from Shah Alam Stadium to the Shah Alam campus of Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM) ahead of the Semiconductor @ UiTM Initiative launch, said the rail service would provide significant benefits to students commuting within the Klang Valley. He said he observed many students using the service and welcomed their positive response.

items.title: From RM85 to RM30: Government picks up the health check tab for truckers to keep roads safe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Effective July 1, commercial vehicle drivers in Malaysia will be eligible for a subsidised medical examination when they renew their vocational licence annually. Currently, drivers are required to pay RM85 for the medical evaluation. Loke said the initiative, implemented under Budget 2026, costs RM12.4 million, comprising RM7 million from Socso, while the Transport Ministry will bear the remaining RM5.4 million.

items.title: Pahang fitness trainer sets third world record with 18-hour cardio and weightlifting feat
items.summary: LIPIS: Fitness instructor K. Jey Prabakaran Thevar has once again pushed the boundaries of human endurance by setting a new world record for the longest non-stop cardio and weightlifting workout session. https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2024/10/1123544/pahang-fitness-trainer-eyes-guinness-world-record Jey Prabakaran said his latest attempt began about 4am and ended around 11pm on June 28, officially clocking 18 hours and securing entries in the Malaysia Book of Records and the Influencer Book of World Records. Jey Prabakaran, fondly known as 'Dr Kjey', dedicated his third world record to his late father, Kunasigaran Suppiya Thevar, who passed away in 1999 when Jey Prabakaran was three years old.

items.title: Contract law reform to boost investor confidence, slash commercial disputes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The proposed reform of contract law will increase legal certainty, reduce transaction costs and commercial disputes, said Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said. Azalina, the Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform), delivered a briefing on the final report of the Study on the Reform of Contract Law in Malaysia. Azalina said the government had taken proactive steps to address the Act's shortcomings by establishing the Committee on the Review and Study of Contract Law Reform on Jan 14, 2025, to lead the study and put forward reform recommendations.

items.title: From Henry Gurney to jail: Court swaps teen driver’s reform school for prison term after fatal joyride in Kuching
items.summary: KUCHING, June 30 — The High Court has substituted a teenage boy’s six-year detention at the Henry Gurney School for causing a fatal multi-vehicle crash on Jalan Teo Kui Seng here in January last year with six years’ imprisonment. He was charged under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 with reckless driving causing the deaths of five people: George Herson Andrew, 15, Japin Kemin, 19, Nasrulhakim Mohamad Sedek, 41, Caryn Anne Risly, 35, and Wan Khairul Nizam Wan Abdul Kadir, 41. The court further held that a three-year detention at the reform school would be manifestly inadequate for an offence under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.

items.title: Taiwan raids tech firms in China AI chip smuggling probe
items.summary: TAIPEI: Taiwanese investigators have raided the Taiwan offices of US company Super Micro Computer and two other tech firms, a prosecutor said Tuesday, as part of an expanded probe into the alleged smuggling of Nvidia AI chips to China. Prosecutors said in May they were investigating the shipment of "high-end" AI servers containing advanced Nvidia chips to China, Macau and Hong Kong, in violation of US export controls. They are accused of forging documents so they could ship roughly 50 servers made by Super Micro Computer to China.

items.title: Amir: Health Ministry budget rationalisation will not affect healthcare reforms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The rationalisation of the Health Ministry's operating expenditure will not affect the implementation of the Medical and Health Insurance/Takaful (MHIT) Plan or the RESET strategy. Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said the proposed adjustment involved only non-critical expenditure and would not affect the ministry's core spending approved under the 2026 Budget. He said the Health Ministry had revised the proposed adjustment to RM500 million in operating expenditure, down from the original proposal of RM3.1 billion.

items.title: Russia pounds on the gates of Ukraine's 'fortress belt'
items.summary: RUSSIA is grinding its way into Kostiantynivka, a key stronghold in Ukraine's eastern "fortress belt" long coveted by the Kremlin, even as its gains across the rest of the 1,200-km front line have largely stalled. Small groups of Russian soldiers are attempting to infiltrate its outskirts, senior Ukrainian commanders said last week, suggesting close-quarters assaults could follow. The push towards it underscores Moscow's enduring manpower advantage, even as Ukrainian mid-range drone strikes on logistics have weakened its fighting capabilities, analysts said.

items.title: Semiconductor@UiTM launched to boost tech talent through industry-integrated learning
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Higher Education Ministry has expressed confidence that Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM) is capable of implementing a major agenda in expanding the semiconductor field through the launch of the Semiconductor@UiTM Initiative. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said the semiconductor field is a high-impact programme and difficult, yet UiTM has shown serious commitment to its implementation. Zambry said this in his speech while officiating the Launching Ceremony of the Semiconductor@UiTM Initiative at UiTM Shah Alam here today.

items.title: Malaysians injured in Thai bomb blast were not intended targets
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The two Malaysians injured in a bomb blast in Tak Bai, Narathiwat, were not the intended targets of the attack, which Thai police believe was carried out by a separatist group. Narathiwat provincial police commander Major General Prayong Kotsakha said investigators believed the improvised explosive device had been planted for another vehicle. He said investigations were ongoing.

items.title: Ghost in the machine: Kota Kinabalu City Hall says parking on the house after app breaks down
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 30 — Motorists using public parking spaces in the city will not be penalised for unpaid parking fees following a temporary disruption affecting the Sabah Smart Parking (SSP) application. In a notice issued on Tuesday, DBKK Holdings Sdn Bhd said the SSP application is currently experiencing operational issues, affecting several key services relied upon by users for daily parking transactions. The temporary suspension effectively provides a parking fee holiday for motorists using city parking bays managed through the SSP system until the application resumes normal operations.

items.title: ‘Think wisely, Johoreans’: A vote for BN could mean a vote for PAS, says PKR sec-gen
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 29 — Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) secretary-general Datuk Fuziah Salleh today said Johoreans should keep in mind that voting for Umno-Barisan Nasional (BN) in the upcoming state election could also translate into support for Parti Islam Se-Malaysia (PAS). “Johor voters need to think wisely. Fuziah said PAS’ move reflected divisions within PN, which she said would benefit Pakatan Harapan (PH).

items.title: Bank Negara: Malaysia's official reserve assets at US$130.63bil at end-May 2026
items.summary: Bank Negara Malaysia said other foreign currency assets amounted to US$766.5 million. These projected foreign currency inflows amount to US$3.04 billion over the next 12 months. "Bank Negara also does not engage in foreign currency options vis-à-vis the ringgit," the central bank said.

items.title: Three NGOs urge government to halt Cybercrimes Bill, seek PSSC review
items.summary: The NGOs said the bill lacked prior judicial authorisation for surveillance, data access, searches and seizures. The three bodies said the bill must include clear human rights protections and safeguards that balance security and fundamental rights. "Access without thorough, independent judicial oversight violates privacy rights.

items.title: Under eight minutes just the start: Transport minister promises shorter wait time for LRT3 trains if Shah Alam crowds surge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Prasarana will gradually increase the frequency of trains operating along the LRT3 Line once the demand picks up and after the new batch of trains arrive, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said today. He said the frequency of trains along the Shah Alam Line will be revised based on the trend of usage after it operates for one whole month. “Currently, the frequency stands about 7.5 minutes at peak hours.

items.title: Johor Umno rubbishes claim BN will win fewer than 40 seats
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Allegations that Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) is faltering are merely political narratives detached from reality, said Johor Umno information chief Md Israk Abdullah. DAP has failed to reflect the true reality at the grassroots level," he said. The people judge based on performance, not propaganda," he said.

items.title: Over 58,000 buildings likely damaged or destroyed in Venezuela
items.summary: CARACAS: The powerful twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela last week damaged or destroyed more than 58,000 buildings, according to a preliminary assessment of satellite data published by US space agency NASA. "Approximately 58,870 buildings were likely damaged or destroyed across the affected region" based on satellite radar data gathered on June 25, the day after the earthquakes, according to researchers Corey Scher and Jamon Van Den Hoek of Oregon State University. The duo were citing data from the European Space Agency's high-resolution radar imagery satellite Sentinel-1.

items.title: MPs trade blows in Dewan Rakyat over Kelantan child sexual crime statistics
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A heated exchange broke out in the Dewan Rakyat after a member of parliament was accused of citing outdated statistics to claim child sexual crime cases were on the rise in Kelantan. The shouting match began when RSN Rayer (PH-Jelutong) defended remarks he made yesterday, insisting that reports he had cited — one of which was from the New Straits Times — showed an increase in sexual crime cases in Kelantan. When I debated yesterday, I was referring to published reports — one by the New Straits Times, one by Free Malaysia Today — which showed statistics of sexual offence cases in Kelantan that are increasing.

items.title: Eightfold surge in deepfakes: Deputy communications minister reveals scale of Malaysia’s AI war
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — More than 11,600 items of false or deepfake content have been taken down from nearly 12,500 complaints related to AI misuse received by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) since 2024, the Dewan Rakyat was told today. Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said the deepfake content was removed following takedown requests submitted by MCMC to social media platform providers. To curb the growing misuse of AI, Teo said the government, through the Risk Mitigation Code (RMC) under the Online Safety Act 2025, now requires licensed social media platforms to put in place risk mitigation measures, including those targeting AI-generated content.

items.title: Thai opposition leader goes on trial over royal insult law
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thailand's main opposition leader and nine other MPs were among dozens on trial Tuesday, accused of ethics breaches over attempts to reform the kingdom's strict royal insult law. The 44 defendants, whose trial opened at the Supreme Court, include Natthaphong Ruengpanyawut, whose progressive People's Party came second in February's general election. The National Anti-Corruption Commission (NACC) filed a civil complaint in April to the Supreme Court against 44 current and former politicians, alleging a breach of ethics.

items.title: Johor election: Local council assures fair treatment for all parties
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor Baru City Council (MBJB) is treating all political parties fairly in processing permit applications throughout the 16th Johor state election campaign, its mayor said. Its mayor Datuk Mohd Haffiz Ahmad said the council's licensing department had established clear guidelines and application procedures to ensure the campaign proceeds smoothly. He said this includes permits for the installation of campaign materials, such as flags, banners, posters, buntings and other forms of election advertising, to ensure they comply with the council's guidelines.

items.title: Putrajaya is safe regardless of Johor’s choice, says BN No 2
items.summary: MUAR, June 30 — The results of the 16th Johor state election will not affect the stability of the federal government following the changing landscape and political dynamics of the country, said Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan. Commenting on the socio-economic aspect, Mohamad said political stability under BN was critical to ensure that Johor continued to drive foreign investment inflows, which were now ahead of other states. He said the surge in economic performance would translate into increased state revenue which would ultimately be repatriated to the people.

items.title: Over 127,000 calls made to Talian Kasih since 2022
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Talian Kasih 15999 helpline has received more than 127,000 calls between 2022 and May this year. Deputy Women, Family and Community Development Minister Lim Hui Ying said 9,327 calls, or 7.36 per cent, involved complaints and cases related to domestic violence. "As of May this year, 470 domestic violence complaints have already been received," she told the Dewan Rakyat today.

items.title: Matrix Concepts rolls out RM30mil homeownership campaign
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Matrix Concepts Holdings Bhd has unveiled a homeownership campaign offering more than RM30 million in savings, rewards and prizes to celebrate its 30th anniversary. The Matrix 30A HOME Rush Campaign will run from June 30 to Nov 30, 2026, featuring selected residential developments across Negri Sembilan, Johor and the Klang Valley. "Matrix Concepts has grown alongside our homeowners and communities.

items.title: No new projects, no new promises: PM Anwar draws the line on election handouts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reiterated that the announcement of new projects or policies during election campaigns is prohibited under election laws. He said the restriction is in line with Section 24B of the Election Offences Act 1954. “Government machinery must not be used to announce new projects during the campaign period, whether by city councils, state governments or the Federal Government,” he said during the Minister’s Question Time in the Dewan Rakyat today.

items.title: Palm oil's future hinges on innovation: Noraini Ahmad
items.summary: BANGI: Malaysia's oil palm industry can no longer rely solely on commodity production and must increasingly be driven by technology, innovation and the commercialisation of high-value products. "The future of the oil palm industry no longer relies solely on commodity production," she said at the Malaysian Palm Oil Board (MPOB) Transfer of Technology (TOT) Programme 2026 here today. Noraini said the technology could reduce reliance on subjective manual inspections, speed up fruit grading, improve operational efficiency at palm oil mills and enhance the productivity and competitiveness of Malaysia's oil palm industry.

items.title: Singapore raises electricity tariffs by 17pct due to Middle East crisis
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Singapore state utility SP Group said on Tuesday that it will raise electricity tariffs by 17 per cent in the third quarter of 2026 due to soaring prices of imported natural gas, which accounts for 95 per cent of the city-state's power production. Here are the details: * Electricity tariffs before taxes for households will increase by 17 per cent compared with the previous quarter, amounting to S$0.0464 ($0.036) per kilowatt-hour, due to higher energy costs. * "An increase in the cost of natural gas will lead to an increase in the costs of producing electricity and town gas in Singapore," it added.

items.title: Malaysia secures RM160mil export prospects at Korea expo
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia External Trade Development Corp (Matrade) has exceeded its export target at the Korea Import Expo (KIE) 2026, securing RM160 million in export prospects . This exceeded Matrade's initial target of RM120 million, proving the strong market demand for Malaysian products in the competitive East Asia region. Matrade chief executive officer Abu Bakar Yusof said the exceptional trade leads secured at KIE 2026 underscore the strong appeal of Malaysian products in the South Korean market.

items.title: Johor election: 'Go ahead,' Onn Hafiz brushes off Puad
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has brushed off attacks levelled against him by former Rengit state assemblyman Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi, saying such tactics would not influence voters in the Johor election. The Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman said he believed Puad's remarks would not sway public opinion. Also present were BN candidates Boo Chin Leong (Penggaram), Mohd Yusla Ismail (Senggarang) and Mohd Zaidi Japar (Rengit).

items.title: New Malaysian passport introduces 94 security features, enhanced travel document protection [WATCH]
items.summary: Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the enhanced features include visual, covert and forensic security elements. He said the new passport also introduces a 10-year validity option priced at RM350, while the five-year passport remains at RM200. Zakaria also presented the new passport to the prime minister during the launch.

items.title: Johor polls: Bersama skips manifesto, unveils five-point voter pledge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Parti Bersama Malaysia today unveiled a five-point service pledge for the Johor state election, saying it would focus on commitments it could realistically deliver rather than issuing a traditional election manifesto. According to The Star , party president Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli said the approach reflected Bersama’s position as a new party contesting only 15 state seats and its aim of becoming a credible opposition rather than forming the state government. “This is a state election, and the powers of the Federal and state governments are clearly defined under the Federal Constitution.

items.title: Hope you get the posts you wanted: PKR sec-gen Fuziah tells ex-members who joined MIC
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 30 — The action by a group of PKR leaders and members to leave the party and join MIC recently has been described as “rather strange” by PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh. She said the party’s internal findings indicated that the move was driven by dissatisfaction over not being appointed to positions within the party. On June 28, former Johor PKR State Leadership Council vice-chairman M. Murugan announced that he and about 200 supporters had left the party to join the MIC Iskandar Puteri division.

items.title: Senior citizen charged with sexually assaulting two schoolgirls in mosque storeroom
items.summary: MELAKA: A senior citizen was charged at the Ayer Keroh Sessions Court today with two counts of committing physical sexual assault against two primary schoolgirls. Aziz Mohammad @ Teh Eng Guan, 73, pleaded not guilty to the charges framed under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017. Deputy public prosecutor Rif'ah 'Izzati Abdul Mutalif appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was represented by S.S. Amritpal Singh.

items.title: RM1.65m worth of counterfeit, contraband liquor seized in Klang Valley raids
items.summary: Fuad Nizam KUALA LUMPUR: Police seized RM1.65 million worth of counterfeit and non-duty-paid alcoholic drinks in two raids in Kepong Baru and Bandar Utama last Thursday. The raids were carried out under Op Taring Alpha 2 by the federal police's Internal Security and Public Order Department's Wildlife Crime Bureau/ Special Investigation Intelligence (WCB/PSK), Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry Enforcement Division and the Sentul district police headquarters' Criminal Investigation Department Serious Crime Unit (D9). Zaki said both suspects played important roles in the syndicate's logistics operations around the Klang Valley.

items.title: Tak Bai bomb blast: ‘It’s a miracle we’re still alive,’ says injured Malaysian victim
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Two Malaysians injured in a roadside bomb explosion in Tak Bai in southern Thailand have described their survival as a “miracle” after being transferred to Kelantan for further treatment. According to Harian Metro , one of the victims, Abdullah Syarapi Abd Rahman, 45, said he and his companion were grateful to have survived the blast, which left them injured but alive despite the intensity of the explosion. Abdullah sustained serious injuries, including wounds to his forehead and head, a fractured left thigh bone, and injuries to his left hand, while Yusri suffered minor injuries.

items.title: Johor polls: Pakatan candidate Khuzzan returns to Semerah to revive stalled development agenda
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 30 — After securing victory previously before losing in the last state election, Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Mohd Khuzzan Abu Bakar is making a comeback in Semerah with a clear mission — to fulfil unfinished development commitments and continue initiatives left behind. Mohd Khuzzan described his return in the 16th Johor state election not as an attempt to redeem a defeat, but as an effort to realise aspirations that were interrupted following PH’s exit from the Johor state administration in 2020. Mohd Fared Mohd Khalid (BN-Umno) 2.

items.title: Anwar expected to unveil Johor Pakatan state election manifesto in next few days, says PKR sec-gen
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 30 — The Pakatan Harapan (PH) manifesto for the Johor state election is expected to be announced in a day or two, said PKR secretary-general Datuk Fuziah Salleh. She said that PH chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who is also PKR president, will make the official announcement. Fuziah said that the PH manifesto for the Johor state election touched on the welfare and needs of the people.

items.title: Pakatan candidate Kartiyaini pushes Skudai vision focused on ageing population and family-friendly spaces
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 30 — Being a true native of Skudai gives Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate J. Kartiyaini a distinct edge in the state constituency race, as she knows the local heartbeat, especially when it comes to the needs of senior citizens. “I still live in Skudai, and my parents are still here too. N48: Skudai: 106,805 1.

items.title: Durian trader pleads not guilty over false Facebook post on alleged fuel shipment to Philippines
items.summary: Nurhafizul Badrul Hisham, 34, as the defendant is charged with making and initiating a communication post with the intention of hurting the feelings of others on Facebook via the account “Nurhafizul Badrul Hisham” on April 12. The post was later seen at Tower 1, Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), Cyberjaya at 6.48am, April 13. Nurhafizul paid the bail money.

items.title: Pilot phase for new base medical insurance plan begins July amid healthcare cost reform push, says finance minister II
items.summary: He said the initiative is part of broader efforts to manage escalating medical inflation and private healthcare costs. “The government introduced the RESET strategy as a comprehensive, targeted and high-impact framework to control rising healthcare costs and private medical insurance premiums,” he said in Parliament on Tuesday. Amir Hamzah said the full rollout of the base MHIT plan is expected nationwide in January 2027.

items.title: Syed Saddiq to skip Johor election campaign after Federal Court ruling postponed
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman will sit out the Johor election campaign as the Federal Court has postponed its decision on his appeal against an acquittal in his graft case involving Armada funds. According to Free Malaysia Today, Syed Saddiq said the delay means he will now have to wait another two weeks before the court delivers its ruling, shifting his focus back to his ongoing legal battle. He said the postponement came as a setback after years of court proceedings.

items.title: No need to rush to replace passports, says Immigration D-G as new rollout begins July 1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Malaysians are urged not to rush to replace their existing passports as the rollout of the country’s new passport begins on July 1 in phases, says Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban. He added that passport renewals should only be carried out when necessary, such as when a passport has expired or when a new application is being made. He then said the new passport will first be issued at 14 state-level passport issuing offices in the initial phase, including UTCs.

items.title: New York style in Johor: Puteri Wangsa hopeful Maszlee pivots to pixels to win over Gen Z voters
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 30 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Puteri Wangsa seat, Dr Maszlee Malik, plans to introduce a dedicated mobile application to allow residents to report local issues and submit complaints more efficiently if elected in the 16th Johor state election. Maszlee said a practical, technology-driven approach is needed given the constituency’s large geographical area and diverse population, which ranges from affluent neighbourhoods such as Austin Heights to Felda Ulu Tebrau. He said he hopes to draw inspiration from the community engagement model adopted by New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani, who uses digital platforms alongside social media to gather feedback directly from residents.

items.title: Over 45,000 arrested in online scam crackdown since 2023, says home minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Police have opened 165,501 investigation papers into online scam cases since 2023, with tens of thousands of arrests made and millions of ringgit in assets seized, according to Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail. In the same reply, Saifuddin said the Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) recorded cases involving love scams, e-commerce fraud and investment scams between 2023 and May 2026. Between 2023 and May 31 this year, police opened 18 investigation papers involving such exploitation, arrested 54 individuals, and rescued 178 victims.

items.title: Pakatan candidate for Kukup Cheah Chee Hong banks on JS-SEZ to lift local economy
items.summary: PONTIAN, June 30 — Drawing on his experience as an entrepreneur, Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Kukup seat, Cheah Chee Hong, is determined to ensure residents benefit directly from the economic opportunities generated by the Johor–Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ). Cheah, 48, said Kukup has strong potential to be a key tourism destination, especially with the upcoming operation of the Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link, which is expected to increase tourist arrivals from Singapore into Johor. As part of efforts to stimulate local economic growth, Cheah also proposed establishing a large-scale night market near the Kukup ferry terminal, capable of accommodating up to 200 stalls dedicated to local entrepreneurs.

items.title: With Gunung Pulai at its doorstep, incumbent Arthur Chiong eyes infrastructure push and tourism boost for Bukit Batu
items.summary: KULAI, June 30 — Incumbent Bukit Batu Assemblyman Arthur Chiong Sen Sern is committed to continuing infrastructure development and strengthening the local economy as the core pillars of his agenda to improve the well-being of residents. Beyond infrastructure, Chiong said he aims to strengthen tourism as a new driver of economic growth in Kulai, particularly within the Bukit Batu constituency, through the organisation of activities and the development of more competitive tourism products. N51: Bukit Batu: 49,963 1.

items.title: Anwar defends KPKT Johor project announcement, says no breach of election law over Budget 2026 allocation
items.summary: Anwar said the allocation was for an approved project under Budget 2026, and not a new development. Any new project approved during campaigning period would have been illegal under the Election Offences Act 1954. “On the KPKT project, when I checked (the minister) announced a project that was already approved,” Anwar said during Prime Minister’s Question Time.

items.title: Pakatan’s Simpang Jeram hopeful banks on TVET training to anchor young workers
items.summary: MUAR, June 30 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Simpang Jeram seat Nazri Abd Rahman is determined to empower the Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) agenda as a proactive measure to curb youth migration out of the district if he is given the mandate in the 16th Johor State Election. N13: Simpang Jeram: 41,975 voters 1. Nazri Abd Rahman (PH-Amanah) 2.

items.title: Anwar: Selangor and Penang aside, all states receive higher development funds than they give in tax
items.summary: Selangor’s average tax revenue from 2023 to 2025 was RM43.6 billion. During that same period, the federal government allocation spent on Selangor was RM15 billion, Anwar said. Kedah, also under PAS administration, recorded RM3.7 billion in revenue from federal tax collection, while federal allocations to the state totalled RM9.5 billion.

items.title: Malaysia drafting new national guidelines for non-Muslim cemeteries, crematoria and columbarium planning
items.summary: According to The Star , Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the draft guideline is being developed by the Department of Town and Country Planning Peninsular Malaysia (PLANMalaysia) to strengthen planning standards for burial and cremation facilities nationwide. Nga said the guideline would also set clear distance requirements between crematoria and surrounding developments. Open-air cremation, Nga said, would be permitted in rural or village areas but would not be allowed on peat soil.

items.title: In Dewan Rakyat today: State allocation distribution, project announcement ban among key issues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — The fairness of Federal Government allocations to state governments and the status of the ban on announcing government projects after election nomination day are expected to be among the focus at today’s Dewan Rakyat sitting. According to the Order Paper published on Parliament’s official website, Datuk Seri Doris Sophia Brodi (GPS-Sri Aman) will ask the Prime Minister to explain claims that Federal Government allocations to state governments are unfair and insufficient compared with the revenue collected, as well as the Madani Government’s efforts to improve the allocation mechanism. During the same session, Datuk Seri Dr Shahidan Kassim (PN-Arau) will also ask the Prime Minister whether the prohibition on announcing government allocations or projects after the close of nominations remains in force, and what enforcement mechanisms will be implemented ahead of the Johor, Melaka and Negeri Sembilan state elections.
